feedback.text: I'm concerned about corporations controlling access to art and culture. That includes pulling songs and videos from streaming services and, as others have pointed out, rendering video games unplayable. I understand there are various commercial and legal issues involved, but preservation of art should not be entrusted to corporations. Ideally, enthusiasts should be able to maintain their own copies of any media, but that falls outside the scope of customer protection. What IS within that scope is companies remotely terminating products that the customer paid for with no knowledge of when that might happen. This is overreach on the company's part and a line buried somewhere in the EULA is not nearly sufficient warning.
